description_long: This object is a slender bird with an elongated body and long, thin wings that extend outward in a straight line. The wings are narrow and appear almost needle-like, giving the bird a streamlined and aerodynamic appearance. The head is small and tapers into a pointed beak, while the tail is also long and thin, contributing to the overall sleek silhouette. The legs are not prominently visible, suggesting they are either tucked in or very short relative to the body. The bird's coloration appears to be a uniform light gray or white, with no visible markings or patterns. Its form and proportions suggest a species adapted for fast, agile flight, possibly resembling a tern or swallow.
description: A slender, long-winged bird with a streamlined body, pointed beak, and uniform light coloration. Its wings and tail are thin and elongated, giving it an aerodynamic silhouette.
description_view_0: The bird is seen head-on, showing the narrow body and symmetrical, outstretched wings.
description_view_1: A side angle reveals the extreme length and thinness of the wings, with the body appearing as a small point.
description_view_2: A rear view highlights the tail and the even spread of the wings, mirroring the front view.
description_view_3: Another side angle, opposite to view 1, again emphasizing the long, straight wings and slender profile.
